F_r.label: Radial peaking F_r
F_r.help: Radial hot-channel factor: ratio of peak-to-average pin power. Drives hot-pin clad temperature and DNBR. Typical range 1.2–1.45.

F_z.label: Axial peaking F_z
F_z.help: Axial peaking factor (chopped cosine). Combined with F_r for hot-pin heat flux. Typical fresh-core range 1.45–1.65.

total_power.label: Rated power [MW]
total_power.help: Rated thermal power. Uncertainty reflects calorimetric measurement error (~1–2% of rated). Modelled as normal.

temp_core_exit.label: Core-exit temp [°C]
temp_core_exit.help: Initial bulk coolant temperature at core exit. Affects initial enthalpy and moderator reactivity feedback. Uncertainty reflects thermocouple calibration and flow mixing (~±3°C).

diameter_break.label: Break diameter [m]
diameter_break.help: Equivalent break diameter. Drives blowdown rate and peak clad temperature. Use a uniform distribution to represent break spectrum uncertainty.

Cd_sub.label: Break Cd (subcooled)
Cd_sub.help: Discharge coefficient for subcooled critical flow at the break. Accounts for vena contracta and non-equilibrium effects. Range 0.85–1.0 per NUREG validation studies.

k_fuel.label: Fuel conductivity [W/m·K]
k_fuel.help: UO₂ thermal conductivity. Decreases with burnup and temperature. Controls fuel centreline temperature and stored energy. Typical BOL ~3 W/m·K; EOL ~2.5 W/m·K.

h_gap.label: Gap conductance [W/m²·K]
h_gap.help: Fuel-clad gap conductance — the largest single contributor to fuel temperature uncertainty. Depends on gap size, fill gas, and contact pressure. Wide uniform range reflects as-fabricated variation.

cp_fuel.label: Fuel Cp [J/kg·K]
cp_fuel.help: UO₂ specific heat capacity. Increases with temperature. Controls the rate of fuel temperature rise during a transient.

lambda_clad.label: Clad conductivity [W/m·K]
lambda_clad.help: Zircaloy-4 thermal conductivity. Relatively insensitive to temperature in the operating range. Uncertainty ~±15%.

htc_post_chf.label: Bromley HTC cap [W/m²·K]
htc_post_chf.help: Upper limit on the Bromley film-boiling HTC after CHF. Represents uncertainty in the inverted-annular flow regime at high pressure. Lower values give more conservative PCT.

htc_core_mult.label: Core HTC multiplier
htc_core_mult.help: Multiplicative factor on the forced-convection (Dittus-Boelter / Churchill-Chu) core HTC during normal pre-CHF operation. Values < 1 reduce cladding-to-coolant heat transfer, raising clad temperature and advancing DNB onset. Conservative direction: < 1. Typical UA range ±20% (0.8–1.2).

htc_fb_mult.label: Film boiling HTC multiplier
htc_fb_mult.help: Multiplicative factor on the post-CHF film boiling HTC — applied to both the Bromley (inverted-annular) result and the steam Dittus-Boelter (reflood / uncovery) result before the htc_post_chf cap is enforced. Values < 1 worsen post-CHF cooling and raise PCT. Conservative direction: < 1. Wide range reflects factor-of-2 scatter in film boiling data.

rewet_dT_K.label: Leidenfrost ΔT [K]
rewet_dT_K.help: Maximum cladding superheat above T_sat at which rewetting (return to nucleate boiling) is permitted. Higher values delay rewetting and produce higher PCT.

_section_flarecon._note: ─── FLARECON containment parameters (only shown when FLARECON sheet exists) ───

condensation_multiplier.label: Condensation model multiplier
condensation_multiplier.help: Multiplier on the wall condensation heat flux — applied uniformly to whichever model is selected (Uchida 1965 or DLM-FM). For Uchida, this scales the empirical HTC directly; for DLM-FM it scales the final mass-transfer-derived heat flux. Accounts for uncertainty in non-condensable suppression of steam condensation, surface wetting behaviour, and condensate film effects. Values < 1 are conservative (reduce heat removal, raise peak pressure).

conc_k.label: Concrete conductivity [W/m·K]
conc_k.help: Thermal conductivity of the reinforced concrete biological shield behind the steel liner. Controls the rate of long-term heat absorption into the concrete mass and therefore the rate of pressure decay after peak. Typical range for structural concrete: 1.0–2.5 W/(m·K) depending on aggregate type, moisture content, and temperature. Lower values are conservative (less long-term heat removal, slower pressure decay).

gap_thick_m.label: Liner–concrete gap [m]
gap_thick_m.help: Thickness of the air gap between the steel liner inner surface and the concrete wall behind it. This gap resistance is the largest single uncertainty in the liner-to-cmPoweroncrete thermal path. A well-bonded liner has near-zero gap; construction imperfections or thermal cycling can open gaps up to ~3 mm. Larger gaps reduce heat conduction to the concrete and raise peak pressure. Conservative direction: larger values.

pcc_fraction.label: PCC/outer cooling fraction
pcc_fraction.help: Fraction of wall area with active outer-surface cooling (passive containment cooling system or equivalent). Set p1 = p2 = 0 for conventional dry containments with no credited outer cooling (variable is then inactive).

T_outer_C.label: Outer wall temperature [°C]
T_outer_C.help: Temperature of the outer containment surface boundary condition, used when pcc_fraction > 0.
